title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
subtitle: What the Rich Teach Their Kids About Money That the Poor and Middle Class Do Not!
author: Robert T. Kiyosaki
themes: Assets vs Liabilities — the rich buy assets, the poor and middle class buy liabilities they call assets
The Rat Race — trading time for money is a trap
Cashflow Quadrant (E-S-B-I) — move from left to right for true wealth
Financial literacy is not taught in schools
Make money work for you, not the other way around
Embrace risk and financial education over job security
Corporations as a vehicle for wealth protection
awards: New York Times Bestseller (over 6 years on list)
USA Today #1 Money Book (two years running)
Longest-running bestseller on all four Publisher's Weekly reporting lists
excerpt: The single most powerful asset we all have is our mind. If it is trained well, it can create enormous wealth. Rich Dad Poor Dad is the controversial, wildly popular personal finance fable that has changed how millions think about money — and sparked decades of debate about whether its lessons are genius or dangerous.
